We want to ensure we are providing high-quality, engaging sessions for your center. Our goal is to continue doing what works well and adapt any elements that may not be meeting your needs to better suit your center when delivering Mini Monstars sessions.

Hi Steph,

It was so nice speaking with you today, and I am thrilled you enjoyed your first session enough to lock in for the year.

I am happy to confirm that we have increased your session time to 50 minutes at a cost of $210 + GST per session. This will include 20 minutes for Junior Kindy and 30 minutes for Kindy. Please note that sessions will continue through school holidays.

I am also pleased to confirm you are all set with Coach Hannah, continuing fortnightly from your original start date. We are so glad to have you with us!
As a friendly reminder, we maintain a four-session notice policy for any cancellations.

I will follow up with you after your first session to gather your feedback, but please feel free to reach out to us at any time prior if you have questions.


Kind Regards,

Hi Viki,

 

Thank you for letting us know. I’ve just tried and our phones seem to be back online now.

 

Farrah has been amazing, very accommodating to children during sports (flexible where needed and allows them to rejoin experience when children wish). She is always happy upon arrival and eager to set up and begin the sports program. Farrah is flexible with our centre needs and children.

 

The sports so far have been great, except for grid-iron. As this isn’t commonly known or played in Australia, the children and educators were not familiar with it, however it challenged the children’s ability follow instructions, learn something new and expand their physical skills.  

 

Please let me know if there is anything else.

 

Warm Regards,

Thank you for a great session today! I hope the kids enjoyed their bike day.
As you would have noticed, Dakota has attended the last two sessions with me at your centre. Recently I have taken on more leadership tasks and require more time for them. I have included Dakotas Blue card WWC please let me know if you require additional information for your register.

Hi  Monique,

I am very sorry to hear your feedback, as we pride ourselves on the high quality of our programs and their alignment with the ELF.

I appreciate you providing such comprehensive details. To ensure your next session meets your expectations, I will send one of my leaders, Coach Aisa to attend the session with Fanta. They will provide support and ensure the program is being delivered to our required standards, specifically regarding the level of engagement and "upskilling" for the older children and ensure it is meeting your needs.
If there are specific skills you expected the children to learn in these first four weeks, please share them with me. We can then review where those skills fit into the 46 week program and shuffling some more advanced skills forward provided the children are demonstrating the required safety and foundational skills for progression.
I look forward to hearing about the next session .
I have attached Aisa's WWC Blue Card below.
 

Kind Regards,

Katie

GC&E Rochedale South
Jun 18, 2026, 3:05 PM (1 day ago)
to me

Hi Katie,

 

Thanks for your email, and I’m pretty sure that I gave an emailed feedback the last time.

 

Offhand, I’m not impressed that the teacher was late again – arrived 9:40am. While I understand that last week she was still looking for the Centre, but today nothing has changed. We try to get the children organised to be ready for the activity before 9:30am, but this does not seem to matter as your teacher is late.

 

I was also expecting an improvement in the activity, and because you said that she will need to build on the skills of the children. The children in Kindergarten 1 did the same activities as with last week, which were much suited for smaller children. These activities were too easy for the older children. I didn’t see any “upskilling” in terms of what the children could do or explore.

 

It is unlikely that we will continue with the program if we do not see an improvement in the content and/or delivery.  I hope there is more to this gymnastics program apart from balancing bean bags on children’s heads.

 

Thanks again for your support.

 

 

Kind regards,

 

Monique Orendain

Centre Manager

patterns.prevention:
• **Assign** a primary coach to each centre with a named backup to ensure consistent staff continuity and eliminate coverage gaps.

• **Audit** all centres with multiple coach rotations (4+ coaches in one program) and consolidate to maximum two coaches per centre.

• **Create** a 2-week notice protocol requiring centres to confirm coach availability before sessions are scheduled.

• **Review** centres merged with adjacent programs within the last 60 days and conduct direct outreach to confirm satisfaction and coach consistency.

patterns.prevention:
• **Audit** coach engagement and energy levels at all centres within 2 weeks—prioritize centres with new or recently assigned coaches.

• **Establish** a backup coach protocol so centres are never without coverage; communicate replacement plans to clients immediately if coach changes are planned.

• **Review** program content and equipment at flagged centres; confirm activities are age-appropriate and challenging enough to retain parent satisfaction.

• **Schedule** monthly check-ins between state managers and centre directors to catch satisfaction issues before cancellation notices arrive.

patterns.prevention:
• Contact centres monthly (not just at renewal) to understand program satisfaction and catch dissatisfaction early before they commit to alternatives.

• Conduct exit interviews within 48 hours of cancellation notice to identify whether switching was cost-driven, scheduling-related, or strategic — then categorize by type to spot patterns.

• Audit pricing and scheduling flexibility against competitor offerings and internal capacity to address the two most common friction points evident in these cancellations.

• Develop a 60-day pre-renewal conversation framework with key accounts to surface concerns and propose adjustments before centres lock in alternative providers.

centres.closedDesc:
Affinity  Group -direction to cut all incursions immediately at center as already over budget. waived 4 session cancellation to assist with confirmation in writing we will be preferred provider as soon as incursions are back email confirmation

Date Started: 28/11/2025
Number of Sessions Completed: 22
Sessions Missed: 0
Rescheduled: 0

Coaches: Mataus ,Patsy

Summary of their time with us: 
The first week of sessions was described as going well, with the coach, Mateu  noted for being polite, professional, and on time, and there were no concerns raised at this early stage. As the program moved into December 2025, feedback remained stable and positive, with no additional bookings requested at that point but still no issues identified, reinforcing that the program had been well received from the outset.

In January 2026, minor operational adjustments were made to better suit the centre’s schedule, including trialling session times between 1:00 pm and 2:30 pm, which were later confirmed as workable. This period reflected a practical alignment phase where the service was being fine-tuned to fit the centre’s routine while maintaining strong satisfaction. By February 2026, direct feedback from the centre continued to be highly positive, with Mateus again described as “great” and “amazing,” consistently on time, and with no complaints raised, indicating growing confidence in the program’s reliability.

In March 2026, further internal coordination took place, including a confirmed adjustment to a 2:00 pm start time, showing continued responsiveness to centre needs. Around the same time, broader feedback highlighted very strong satisfaction, with both coaches Mateus Gomes and Patricia (Patsy) Del Valle being praised for their energy, engagement, and ability to connect with children. Staff noted that the children were actively enjoying the sessions, and the coaches were described as having positive, bubbly personalities and being highly attentive and professional.

By April 2026, the program had fully stabilised and was operating at a consistently high level, with explicit feedback confirming that Mateus was engaging well with children and delivering sessions that were both enjoyable and well-received. Staff reiterated that children loved the activities and that the coach was a strong positive influence in the program, with no concerns raised at this stage.

In May 2026, feedback continued to reflect satisfaction with the program, with the centre confirming they were happy with the sessions and the coach, although Aletia was noted as being away at the time. Shortly after, the centre made the decision to cancel the program due to budget constraints rather than any issues with delivery or satisfaction. The final communication acknowledged that the service had been well liked and that both the coach and sessions had been appreciated, with an indication that they may reconnect in future when budget allows.

Overall, the timeline reflects a consistently positive service experience from start to finish, characterised by strong coach performance, high child engagement, smooth operational adjustments, and stable satisfaction, with the only reason for discontinuation being external financial constraints rather than program quality or delivery issues.

Next point of contact: purely budget happy to have us back asap when they have budget

preCloseStats.tone_reason: Centre is very happy with coaches Mateus and Patsy; children enjoying sessions with no concerns raised.

Following the Christmas period, sessions resumed in January 2026, with final schedule details confirmed on 20 January. On 28 January 2026, more detailed feedback was received from the centre regarding Coach Mateus, with educators noting that while he was well-liked and engaged positively with children, sessions appeared repetitive, lacked visible progression between age groups, and required stronger structure and preparation.
